title: breynia spatulifolia
intro: Breynia spatulifolia, also known as the "Croton Codon" or "Lampayo", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Breynia spatulifolia include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to address various health concerns.
